# Canny vs Featurebase 2026: Which Feedback Tool Wins? (fdback.io)

## Summary
Head-to-head comparison of Canny vs Featurebase focusing on pricing model differences (per-tracked-user vs per-seat), integration depth, AI feature costs, and use-case fit. Also notes Canny renamed its plans in 2026 (legacy "Starter/Growth" names are now obsolete). Most relevant for the B2B SaaS team choosing between the two most popular incumbent tools.

## Key quotations / statistics
- **Pricing model difference:** "Canny charges per tracked user — anyone who votes, comments, or submits feedback counts. Featurebase charges per admin seat — team members who manage feedback. End users are unlimited."
- **Canny AI:** AI-powered duplicate detection, smart replies, feedback summarization — included on paid plans at no extra cost.
- **Featurebase AI:** Fibi AI agent for support conversations, AI copilot, AI replies — but charged at **$0.29 per AI resolution** plus $19/agent/mo add-on.
- **Integration depth:** "Canny has deeper, more mature two-way integrations with Jira, Linear, Intercom, Salesforce, Zendesk, and more. Featurebase has good integrations but fewer options."
- **Featurebase differentiator:** Full support suite — unified inbox, AI agent, help center up to 50 articles on free plan. Featurebase is trying to consolidate feedback + support into one tool.
- **Plan name update (2026):** "Canny renamed plans in 2026, so older comparisons referencing 'Starter' and 'Growth' are outdated. Always check canny.io/pricing before signing." New names: Free, Core at $19/mo, Pro at $79/mo, Business custom.
- Cost crossover: "Featurebase is cheaper for solo founders (free or $29/mo for 1 seat). Canny is cheaper for teams of 3+ people with moderate user engagement."
- **Featurebase strategic risk (2026):** "Featurebase has been shifting focus toward becoming a live chat platform. That's great if you need chat, but feedback features may not get the same attention going forward."

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- **Critical for `guides/00-platform-selection.md`** - the pricing model difference (per-user vs per-seat) is the #1 decision factor at scale.
- The Featurebase strategic pivot toward live chat (2026) is a significant risk factor for teams choosing it as a primary feedback tool. This warrants a dedicated caveat in the platform selection guide.
- Canny's AI features being included at no extra cost (vs Featurebase's per-resolution charges) is a concrete differentiator for high-volume teams.
- The plan renaming note is a hygiene flag: the stinger should link to live pricing pages, not embed specific plan names.
- The Featurebase unified inbox / support suite overlap with `live-chat-support-worker-bee` should be noted as a boundary case.
